content.text:
```mdl
@ Contract: Confidentiality and Non‑Disclosure Agreement (CNDA)
@ Module name required by the user
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_10_nda_17

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Imports
# ------------------------------------------------------------
open std.collections   # for List<T>

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Types – domain concepts appearing in the CNDA
# ------------------------------------------------------------

@ Parties to the agreement
type Party = {
    name: string               # e.g. "Canadian Pacific Railway", "Supplier"
}

@ Information that is identified as confidential
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    marked_confidential: bool  # true when the information is labelled “Confidential”
    source: Party              # the Disclosing Party that supplied the information
    is_disclosed: bool         # true if the Receiving Party has disclosed it to a third‑party
    is_returned: bool          # true if the information has been returned/destroyed on request
}

@ Information that may be retained in the unaided memory of a person
type ResidualInfo = {
    description: string        # free‑form description of the residual knowledge
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – stable public objects that can be aligned across modules
# ------------------------------------------------------------

@ The whole CNDA agreement (the “contract”)
entity cnda: {
    disclosing_party: Party,
    receiving_party: Party,
    confidential_infos: List<ConfidentialInfo>,
    residual_infos: List<ResidualInfo>,
    termination_date: string,        # ISO‑date of termination (if any)
    request_return: bool            # true when the Disclosing Party has asked for return
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions – keep rules readable
# ------------------------------------------------------------

@ True whether a piece of information is confidential
func is_confidential(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    info.marked_confidential

@ True when a piece of confidential information has been disclosed
func has_been_disclosed(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    info.is_disclosed

@ True when a piece of confidential information has been returned/destroyed
func has_been_returned(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    info.is_returned

@ True when a piece of residual information exists
func residual_exists(c: { residual_infos: List<ResidualInfo> }) -> bool:
    list.len(c.residual_infos) > 0

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – deontic constraints derived from the CNDA text
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Article 2.03 – “Kept in Confidence”
@ 2.03 Kept in Confidence
rule O keep_confidential_information:
    # The Receiving Party must keep every confidential item with at least
    # a reasonable degree of care (the rule is an obligation; the exact
    # quantitative care level is not modelled here).
    true always

# Article 2.05 – “No Disclosure”
@ 2.05 No Disclosure
rule F disclose_confidential_information:
    let any_disclosed: bool =
        exists i: ConfidentialInfo in cnda.confidential_infos where
            is_confidential(i) and has_been_disclosed(i)
    any_disclosed false always

# Article 2.07 – “Return of Confidential Information”
@ 2.07 Return of Confidential Information
rule O return_confidential_on_request:
    let any_not_returned: bool =
        exists i: ConfidentialInfo in cnda.confidential_infos where
            cnda.request_return and not has_been_returned(i)
    any_not_returned false always

# Article 2.08 – “Responsibility for Others”
@ 2.08 Responsibility for Others
rule O responsibility_for_representatives:
    # The Receiving Party is responsible for any breach caused by its
    # Representatives.  This is modelled as an obligation that must hold
    # at all times.
    true always

# Article 3.02 – “Continuing Obligations Regarding Confidentiality”
@ 3.02 Continuing Obligations
rule O obligations_survive_three_years:
    # The confidentiality obligations survive three years after termination.
    # We model the survival as an obligation that is active while the
    # current date is within three years of the termination date.
    # (The actual date arithmetic is omitted – the rule simply states
    # the intent that the obligation is perpetual for the required period.)
    true always

# Article 3.11 – “Residuals”
@ 3.11 Residual Information
rule P use_residual_information:
    # The Receiving Party may use residual information “during and after the term”.
    # This is a permission that is always available (the clause does not
    # impose any further condition).
    residual_exists(cnda) always

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Hypothesis – “Receiving Party may create a copy of some Confidential
# Information in some circumstances.”
# ------------------------------------------------------------
@ This rule captures the hypothesis that a copy (i.e., a residual
@ representation) may be created.  The permission granted by clause 3.11
@ allows the Receiving Party to retain such a copy in its unaided memory.
rule P create_copy_of_residual_information:
    # The permission is granted whenever residual information exists.
    residual_exists(cnda) always
```
